design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore nonlinear map as Mikey, rescue Goonies from first-person adventure rooms, acquire equipment to access new areas, defeat Fratellis and free Annie
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Persistent Fratelli family pursuit during platforming; obscure item acquisition requirements and implicit progression clues create discovery-driven tension
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Mikey from The Goonies film, rescuing captured friends through exploration, puzzle-solving, and combat in a sprawling hideout
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid platform-adventure design with intentionally obscure progression (e.g., hitting specific NPC five times to get candle); game design relies on implicit clues and strategic absence of guidance rather than explicit direction; emphasizes exploration and discovery over clarity

difficulty.lives_system: Health meter drains from enemy contact or environmental damage; one life lost when health fully depleted or falling off screen; game over when all lives lost
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows resuming with retained items and progress; no traditional continue feature
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based checkpoint system; no mid-level checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: None documented; no difficulty selection or assist modes noted

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment-gated progression: primary weapons (yo-yo, slingshot, boomerang), secondary weapons (bomb, molotov cocktail), special items (spring shoes for jumping, hyper shoes for speed, diving suit for underwater, glasses for hidden doors, ladder, candle, key, Magic Locator Device). Each rescued Goonie increases Mikey's health.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Door and ladder navigation between zones; items found in first-person adventure rooms unlock platform areas; rescuing Goonies gates access to Annie
replayability_hooks: Password system enables resumption with progression preserved
Multiple weapon types offer varied approaches to combat encounters
Hidden items and obscure acquisition methods reward exploration and experimentation
Goonies randomizer available in modding community

world_building_techniques: First-person adventure mode rooms establish hideout geography and atmosphere
NPC interactions in adventure rooms provide exposition and hints
Rescued Goonies serve as narrative milestones and health upgrades
Zone-specific enemies and graphics reinforce distinct area identities
Title sequence and opening cutscene establish film context

boss_design.philosophy: Fratelli family members (Jake, Francis, Cousin Pipsqueak, Ma Fratelli) act as persistent recurring obstacles rather than traditional boss encounters; they pursue Mikey throughout platforming, can be stunned but not defeated, creating attrition-based tension
notable_bosses: Cousin Pipsqueak - vanishes when hit, distinct mechanic

faction_indicators: Fratelli family as antagonist faction (Jake, Francis, Pipsqueak, Ma Fratelli)
Goonies as rescue faction (six named individuals plus Annie)
danger_signals: Fratelli family pursuit during platform sections
Health meter depletion as immediate threat
Fall hazards and screen-bottom death pit
environmental_storytelling: Hideout zones with distinct visual themes and enemy types establish Fratelli lair geography
Cell areas in first-person mode where Goonies are imprisoned
Strategic item placement guides implicit progression narrative
Transceiver tool enables hidden communication with game characters and Konami staff (proto-version)
